blocks:
Sūrat al-Ḥāqqah

Meccan. Its number of verses is fifty-one verses according to the Basran and Damascene counts, and two verses in the remaining counts. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الْحَاقَّةُ ** (1) الْحَاقَّةُ (The Inevitable Calamity.) It is said: The Hour (الساعة) whose occurrence is inevitable (يَحِقُّ وُقُوعُهَا), or in which matters become realized (تَحِقُّ فِيهَا الْأُمُورُ), meaning they become necessary (تَجِبُ) and their realities are known, or in which the inevitable realities (حَوَاقُّ الْأُمُورِ) of matters, such as the reckoning (الْحِسَاب) and the recompense (الْجَزَاء), occur.

(٢) مَا الْحَاقَّةُ ** (2) مَا الْحَاقَّةُ (What is the Inevitable Calamity?) Meaning: What thing is it? The explicit noun (الظاهر) is placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) to magnify its status (تَفْخِيمًا لِشَأْنِهَا) and to instill awe of it (تَهْوِيلًا لَهَا).

(٣) وَما أَدْراكَ مَا الْحَاقَّةُ ** (3) وَما أَدْرَاكَ مَا الْحَاقَّةُ (And what will show you what the Inevitable Calamity is?) And what thing informed you of what it is? Meaning that you do not know its essence (كُنْهَهَا), for it is greater than what knowledge (دِرَايَة) can reach.

(٤) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ وَعادٌ بِالْقارِعَةِ ** (4) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ وَعَادٌ بِالْقَارِعَةِ (Thamud and ‘Ad denied the Striking Calamity.) [They denied] the state that strikes (تَقْرَعُ) people with frights (الْإِفْزَاع) and terrors (الْأَهْوَال), and strikes the celestial bodies (الْأَجْرَام) with splitting (الِانْفِطَار) and scattering (الِانْتِشَار). The word الْحَاقَّةُ (the Inevitable Calamity) was placed in the position of the pronoun [referring to it] as an increase in the description of its severity.

(٥) فَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِالطَّاغِيَةِ ** (5) فَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِالطَّاغِيَةِ (As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the outrageous [calamity].) [They were destroyed] by the event (الْوَاقِعَة) that exceeded the limit (الْمُجَاوِزَة لِلْحَدّ) in severity, which is the shout (الصَّيْحَة) and the tremor (الرَّجْفَة), as its explanation preceded in Sūrat al-A‘rāf and Sūrat Hūd.

(٦) وَأَمَّا عادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِرِيحٍ صَرْصَرٍ ** (6) وَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِرِيحٍ صَرْصَرٍ (As for ‘Ad, they were destroyed by a howling wind,) Al-Qummiyy said: meaning cold (بَارِدَة). عاتِيَةٍ ** (fierce). He said: It came out more than what it was commanded.

(٧) سَخَّرَها عَلَيْهِمْ ** (7) سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيَالٍ وَثَمَانِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومًا (which He unleashed upon them for seven nights and eight days continuously.) Allah imposed it upon them by His power (سَلَّطَهَا اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِقُدْرَتِهِ). سَبْعَ لَيالٍ وَثَمانِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُوماً ** (for seven nights and eight days continuously) Successive (مُتَتَابِعَات). Al-Qummiyy said: The moon was afflicted by Saturn (مَنْحُوسًا بِزُحَل) for seven nights and eight days until they perished.

I say: It has preceded in Sūrat al-Qamar that the first and the last of the eight [days] were Wednesday, and that it was a continuous ill-omen (نَحْس مُسْتَمِرّ). فَتَرَى الْقَوْمَ فِيها صَرْعى (So you might see the people therein lying prostrate) Dead (مَوْتَى), the plural of صَرِيع (prostrate one). كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجازُ نَخْلٍ ** (as if they were the trunks of uprooted palms) The roots of palm trees (أُصُول نَخْل). خاوِيَةٍ ** (hollow) Whose insides are decayed (مُتَآكِلَة الْأَجْوَاف).

٨ فَهَلْ تَرى لَهُمْ مِنْ باقِيَةٍ ** (8) Do you see any remnant of them? Their story has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf and Surat Hud.

٩ وَجاءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَنْ قَبْلَهُ وَالْمُؤْتَفِكاتُ بِالْخاطِئَةِ ** (9) And Pharaoh came, and those who were before him, and the overturned cities, with the error. ** وَجاءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَنْ قَبْلَهُ ** (And Pharaoh came, and those who were before him) means: and those who preceded him. And it was read (وقرئ) as وَمَنْ قِبَلَهُ (and those from his side), meaning: those who were with him from his followers. وَالْمُؤْتَفِكاتُ (and the overturned cities) refers to the villages of the people of Lot, and what is meant is their people. بِالْخاطِئَةِ (with the error/sin) means with the error (بالخطإ).

And according to al-Qummi (القمّيّ), الْمُؤْتَفِكاتُ ** (the overturned cities) refers to al-Basrah (البصرة), and الخاطئة (the error/sinner) refers to a certain woman (فلانة).

(١٠) ** فَعَصَوْا رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ ** (10) But they disobeyed the apostle of their Lord, So every nation disobeyed its messenger. فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةً رابِيَةً ** whereupon He seized them with a seizing that was excessive. Exceeding in severity, corresponding to the excess of their deeds in ugliness. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): And al-rābiyah (الرابية) is that which exceeded (رابت) due to what they committed.

(١١) ** إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَى الْماءُ ** (11) Indeed, when the water was rebellious, exceeded its usual limit, meaning during the Flood (الطّوفان), حَمَلْناكُمْ فِي الْجارِيَةِ We carried you in the running [ark]. We carried your fathers while you were in their loins, in the Ark of Noah (سفينة نوح). (١٢) ** لِنَجْعَلَها ** (12) so that We might make it so that We might make the action (الفعلة), which is the deliverance of the believers and the drowning of the disbelievers, لَكُمْ تَذْكِرَةً ** a reminder for you, an admonition (عبرة) and a sign (دلالة) of the Creator's power (قدرة الصانع), His wisdom (حكمته), the perfection of His subjugation (كمال قهره), and His mercy (رحمته), وَتَعِيَها ** and that an attentive ear may retain it. and that it may preserve it, أُذُنٌ واعِيَةٌ ** and that an attentive ear may retain it. whose nature it is to preserve what must be preserved by remembering it, spreading it, reflecting upon it, and acting according to its requirement. And (أُذُنٌ) was read with takhfif (lightening).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He said to Ali عليه السلام: "O Ali, indeed Allah the Almighty commanded me to bring you near and not to keep you far, and to teach you so that you may retain it (تَعِي). And it is a right upon Allah that you retain it." Then **وَتَعِيَها أُذُنٌ واعِيَةٌ ** was revealed.

And in it (al-Majma'), and in al-Uyun and al-Jawami', narrated from him (Ali) عليه السلام: That when this Ayah was revealed, He said: "I asked Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, to make it your ear, O Ali." And in a narration: When it was revealed, He said: "O Allah, make it Ali's ear." Then Ali عليه السلام said: "I never heard anything from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and forgot it." And in another [narration], it adds: "And it was not for me to forget."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When **وَتَعِيَها أُذُنٌ واعِيَةٌ ** was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "It is your ear, O Ali."

footnote (١) Meaning, from an enduring soul (*نفسٍ باقية*), and it is said, from endurance (*بقاء*).

(١٣) فَإِذا نُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ نَفْخَةٌ واحِدَةٌ ** (13) When the Trumpet is blown with a single blast, When He reached the point of terrifying description of the Resurrection (القيامة) and mentioning the fate of those who denied it, He returned to explaining it. What is meant is the first blast (النفخة الأولى) at which the world is destroyed.

(١٤) وَحُمِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ وَالْجِبالُ ** lifted from their places ** فَدُكَّتا دَكَّةً واحِدَةً ** (14) and the earth and the mountains are lifted up, and crushed to pieces with a single crushing, al-Qummi said: They fell, and some of them were crushed upon others.

(١٥) فَيَوْمَئِذٍ ** So, at that time, ** وَقَعَتِ الْواقِعَةُ ** the Resurrection (القيامة) will commence. (15) on that day will the Event occur.

**(١٦) وَانْشَقَّتِ السَّماءُ فَهِيَ يَوْمَئِذٍ واهِيَةٌ ** weak, slackened. (16) The sky will be split, and on that day it will be frail.

(١٧) وَالْمَلَكُ ** And the species known as the angel (الملك) ** عَلى أَرْجائِها ** upon its sides. وَيَحْمِلُ عَرْشَ رَبِّكَ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ ثَمانِيَةٌ** (17) The angels will be on its edges, and on that day eight [angels] will bear the Throne of your Lord above them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Indeed, today they are four, but when the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, He will support them with four others, so they will be eight."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: "The bearers of the Throne (حملة العرش)—and the Throne (العرش) is knowledge (العلم)—are eight: four from us and four from whomever Allah wills." al-Qummi said: "The bearers of the Throne are eight. Each one has eight eyes, and every eye covers the world (طباق الدّنيا)." He said: And in another narration (حديث آخر), he said: "The bearers of the Throne are eight: four from the first generations (الأوّلين) and four from the later generations (الآخرين). As for the four from the first generations, they are Noah (نوح), Abraham (إبراهيم), Moses (موسى), and Jesus (عيسى) عليهم السلام. And as for the four from the later generations, they are Muhammad (محمّد), Ali (عليّ), al-Hasan (الحسن), and al-Husayn (الحسين) صلوات الله عليهم. The meaning of 'they bear the Throne' means they bear the knowledge (العلم)."

(١٨) يَوْمَئِذٍ تُعْرَضُونَ لا تَخْفى مِنْكُمْ خافِيَةٌ ** (18) On that day you will be presented [before Him], no secret of yours will remain hidden. (Meaning: a secret (سريرة)). And it was recited with the yā’ (ي). (١٩) فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ ** (19) As for him who is given his book in his right hand, (This is) a detailed explanation (تفصيل) of the presentation (العرض). فَيَقُولُ ** (he will say,) as a means of proving merit (تحجّجاً) هاؤُمُ اقْرَؤُا كِتابِيَهْ ** (Here, read my book!) هاؤم is a noun (اسم) meaning 'take' (خذوا). And the hā’ (الهاء) in كتابيه and its subsequent counterparts (نظائره الآتية) is for pausing (للسّكت); it is affirmed (تثبت) during pausing (في الوقف) and dropped (تسقط) during continuation (في الوصل).

(٢٠) إِنِّي ظَنَنْتُ ** meaning, I was certain (تيقّنت). This is mentioned thus in al-Tawhid and al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). He said: "And al-ẓann (الظن) is two types: ẓann (ظن) of doubt (شكّ) and ẓann (ظن) of certainty (يقين). So, whatever al-ẓann (الظن) concerns the matter of the Return (المعاد), it is ẓann (ظن) of certainty (يقين), and whatever concerns the matter of this world (الدنيا), it is ẓann (ظن) of doubt (شكّ)." ** أَنِّي مُلاقٍ حِسابِيَهْ ** He said: "Indeed, I shall be resurrected and held to account."

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every nation will be held to account by the Imam of its time. And the Imams (الأئمّة) will recognize their friends (أوليائهم) and their enemies (أعدائهم) by their marks (بسيماهم). And this is His saying: وَعَلَى الْأَعْرافِ رِجالٌ يَعْرِفُونَ ** (46) And on the Elevations will be men who recognize And they are the Imams (peace be upon them). They recognize everyone by their marks (بسيماهم). So they will give their friends (أوليائهم) their book in their right hands, and they will pass into Paradise without reckoning (حساب). And they will give their enemies (أعداءهم) their book in their left hands, and they will pass into the Fire without reckoning (حساب). Then, when their friends (أولياؤهم) look at their book, they will say to their brothers: هاؤُمُ اقْرَؤُا كِتابِيَهْ إِنِّي ظَنَنْتُ أَنِّي مُلاقٍ حِسابِيَهْ.** (19) ‘Here, read my book! (20) Indeed, I knew that I would encounter my account!’

(٢١) فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَةٍ راضِيَةٍ ** (21) he will be in a pleasing life, Al-Qummi (said): meaning (مَرْضِيَّة), so the active participle (الفاعل) was placed in the stead of the passive participle (المفعول). (٢٢) فِي جَنَّةٍ عالِيَةٍ. (22) in a lofty Garden. (٢٣) قُطُوفُها ** (23) Its clusters [of fruit] will be near at hand. is the plural of (قِطْف), which is what is gathered quickly. ** دانِيَةٌ ** the standing and the sitting person submit to it (i.e., it is easily accessible). (٢٤) كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا هَنِيئاً بِما أَسْلَفْتُمْ فِي الْأَيَّامِ الْخالِيَةِ ** (24) ‘Eat and drink to your heart’s content, for what you sent ahead in the days past.’ for what you sent ahead of righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحة) in the past days of the world (الدنيا).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: that a man from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) came to him and said: "O Abu al-Qasim, you claim that the people of Paradise eat and drink?" He said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, verily, the man among them will be given the strength of one hundred men in eating, drinking, and intercourse (الجماع)." He said: "But the one who eats and drinks will have a need (i.e., excretion)." He said: "A sweat that flows like the scent of musk (المسك), and when that occurs, his stomach will shrink (or become slender) for him."

(٢٥) وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِشِمالِهِ ** (25) But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Mu'awiyah. فَيَقُولُ يا لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أُوتَ كِتابِيَهْ. (25) he will say, 'I wish I had not been given my book, (٢٦) وَلَمْ أَدْرِ ما حِسابِيَهْ ** (26) and had not known what my reckoning is! He says this when he sees the evil consequence (سوء العاقبة). (٢٧) يا لَيْتَها ** (27) 'I wish that [death] had been the end of it! I wish the death that I died كانَتِ الْقاضِيَةَ ** (had been the end of it!) meaning: the decisive (القاطعة) end to my affair, so that I would not be resurrected after it. (٢٨) ما أَغْنى عَنِّي مالِيَهْ ** (28) 'My wealth has not availed me! It was said: My wealth (مالي) consisting of property (المال) and followers (التبّع). And al-Qummi means the wealth that he amassed. (٢٩) هَلَكَ عَنِّي سُلْطانِيَهْ ** (29) 'My authority has perished and gone from me!' It was said: My dominion (ملكي) and my dominance (تسلّطي) over the people. And al-Qummi said: That is, his proof (حجّته). (٣٠) خُذُوهُ ** (30) [The command will be:] 'Seize him, It will be said to the keepers of the Fire (خزنة النّار): Seize him فَغُلُّوهُ. (30) and shackle him! (٣١) ثُمَّ الْجَحِيمَ صَلُّوهُ. (31) Then cast him into hell! (٣٢) ثُمَّ فِي سِلْسِلَةٍ ذَرْعُها سَبْعُونَ ذِراعاً فَاسْلُكُوهُ. (32) Then bind him in a chain seventy cubits long! al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If just one link (حلقة واحدة) of the chain, whose length is seventy cubits (سبعون ذراعاً), were placed upon the world (الدنيا), the world would melt (لذابت) from its heat."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Mu'awiyah was the subject of the chain (صاحب السلسلة) about which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فِي سِلْسِلَةٍ ذَرْعُها (in a chain seventy cubits long) [the rest of] the Ayah." He said: "And he was the Pharaoh of this Ummah."

And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "I was behind my father while He was upon His mule. His mule shied away, and behold, there was an old man with a chain around his neck and a man following him. The old man said: 'O Ali ibn al-Husayn, give me water to drink.' The man [following him] said: 'Do not give him water to drink; may Allah not give him water to drink.' He said: 'And that old man was Mu'awiyah.'"

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "He (my father) descended into the valley of Ḍajnan (وادي ضجنان) and said three times: 'May Allah not forgive you.' Then He said to His companions: 'Do you know why I said what I said?' They replied: 'Why did you say it? May Allah make us your ransom (جعلنا الله فداك).' He said: 'Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan passed by me, being dragged in a chain, having let his tongue hang out, asking me to seek forgiveness for him. And it is indeed said that this valley is one of the valleys of Hell (جهنّم).'"

And al-Qummi said: "The inner meaning (في الباطن) of the chain seventy cubits long is the seventy tyrants (الجبابرة السّبعون)."

(٣٣) إِنَّهُ كانَ لا يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ الْعَظِيمِ. (33) Indeed, he would not believe in Allah, the All-great. (٣٤) وَلا يَحُضُ ولا يحثّ عَلى طَعامِ الْمِسْكِينِ. (34) nor urge the feeding of the needy. (٣٥) فَلَيْسَ لَهُ الْيَوْمَ هاهُنا حَمِيمٌ a relative who protects him (قريب يحميه). (35) So he has no friend here today, (٣٦) وَلا طَعامٌ إِلَّا مِنْ غِسْلِينٍ the washing/effluent of the people of the Fire and their pus (غسالة أهل النار وصديدهم). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: the sweat of the disbelievers (عرق الكفّار). (36) nor any food except ghislīn, (٣٧) لا يَأْكُلُهُ إِلَّا الْخاطِؤُنَ the people of sins/errors (اصحاب الخطايا), derived from (من) خطى الرّجل (the man erred) when he committed the sin intentionally (إذا تعمّد الذنب). (37) which none but the errant will eat. (٣٨) فَلا أُقْسِمُ the لا is extra (لا مزيدة) بِما تُبْصِرُونَ. (38) But nay! I swear by what you see, (٣٩) وَما لا تُبْصِرُونَ by the visible things and the unseen things (بالمشاهدات والمغيبات). (39) and what you do not see, (٤٠) إِنَّهُ that it is the Quran (انّ القرآن) لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ upon Allah (على الله), who conveys it from Allah, for the Messenger does not speak on his own behalf (فانّ الرسول لا يقول عن نفسه). The intended meaning (المراد) is either Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله or Gabriel (جبرئيل). (40) that this is indeed the speech of a noble apostle, (٤١) وَما هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شاعِرٍ as you sometimes claim (كما تزعمون تارة) قَلِيلاً ما تُؤْمِنُونَ. (41) and it is not the speech of a poet—little is it that you believe! (٤٢) وَلا بِقَوْلِ كاهِنٍ as you claim at other times (كما تدعون اخرى) قَلِيلاً ما تَذَكَّرُونَ and for that reason, the matter becomes confused for you (ولذلك يلتبس الأمر عليكم). It was said: He mentioned faith (الإيمان) alongside the negation of poetry (نفي الشاعريّة) and remembrance/admonition (التذكّر) alongside the negation of soothsaying (نفي الكاهنيّة), because the lack of similarity between the Quran and poetry is a manifest matter (أمر بيّن) that none denies except an obstinate person (معاند). This is contrary to its distinction from soothsaying (مباينته للكهانة), for knowledge of this distinction depends on remembering the circumstances of the Messenger and the meanings of the Quran that contradict the method of the soothsayers (الكهنة) and the meanings of their sayings. And it was read with the yā’ (بالياء) in both (verbs). (42) Nor the speech of a soothsayer—little is it that you take admonition!

(٤٣) تَنْزِيلٌ مِّن رَّبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ ** (A sending down from the Lord of all the worlds.) ** تَنْزِيلٌ ** (A sending down) refers to it being a sending down مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (from the Lord of all the worlds). He sent it down upon the tongue of Jibrāʾīl (جبرئيل). (٤٤) وَلَوْ تَقَوَّلَ عَلَيْنَا بَعْضَ الْأَقَاوِيلِ ** (And had he fabricated some statements against Us,) Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: He means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. (٤٥) لَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُ بِالْيَمِينِ ** (We would have surely seized him by the right hand,) meaning, by his right hand (بيمينه) or by Our power (بقوّتنا). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: We would have taken vengeance upon him with power (بقوّة). (٤٦) ثُمَّ لَقَطَعْنَا مِنْهُ الْوَتِينَ ** (then We would have surely cut off his life-vein.) meaning, the pericardium (نياط قلبه) of his heart. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It is a vein (عرق) in the back (الظَّهر) from which the child (الولد) originates. This is a depiction (تصوير) of His destruction of him by the most terrible means that kings employ against those with whom they are angered. (٤٧) ** فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ عَنْهُ حَاجِزِينَ ** (and none of you could have withheld [Us] from him.) meaning, defenders (دافعين). This means that he would not undertake to lie against Us for your sake, knowing that if he undertook that, We would punish him, and then you would not be able to repel Our punishment from him. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: This means that no one can restrain Allah (لا يحجز الله أحد) or prevent Him from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(٤٨) وَإِنَّهُ لَتَذْكِرَةٌ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ. (48) Indeed it is a reminder for the Godwary. (٤٩) وَإِنَّا لَنَعْلَمُ أَنَّ مِنْكُمْ مُكَذِّبِينَ. (49) And We certainly know that there are deniers among you. (٥٠) وَإِنَّهُ لَحَسْرَةٌ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ ** (50) Indeed it is a regret for the faithless when they see the reward of those who believe in it. (٥١) وَإِنَّهُ لَحَقُّ الْيَقِينِ ** (51) And indeed it is the truth of certainty, which there is no doubt about. (٥٢) فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ ** (52) So glorify the Name of your Lord, the All-great. So glorify Allah by mentioning His Great Name, declaring Him far removed (تنزيهاً له) from being pleased with fabrication (التقوّل) against Him, and in gratitude for what He revealed to you.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding: إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ ** (Indeed it is the speech of a noble messenger), meaning Gabriel (جبرئيل), from Allah concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). He said: They said that Muhammad lied against his Lord and that Allah did not command him with this concerning Ali. So Allah revealed a Quranic verse regarding that, saying that the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him) is تَنْزِيلٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ وَلَوْ تَقَوَّلَ عَلَيْنا بَعْضَ الْأَقاوِيلِ ** (A sending down from the Lord of all the worlds. And had he fabricated some statements against Us) — the Ayah. Then He turned the discourse and said that the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him) is لَتَذْكِرَةٌ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (a reminder for the Godwary) for all the worlds, and that Ali (peace be upon him) is لَحَسْرَةٌ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ (a regret for the faithless), and that his Wilayah is لَحَقُّ الْيَقِينِ (the truth of certainty). So, O Muhammad, فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ (So glorify the Name of your Lord, the All-great). He says: Thank your Great Lord Who granted you this virtue (الفضل).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله took Ali's hand and declared his Wilayah, they both said: "By Allah, this is not from Allah, nor is this anything but something he wished to honor his cousin with." So Allah revealed: وَلَوْ تَقَوَّلَ عَلَيْنا بَعْضَ الْأَقاوِيلِ ** (And had he fabricated some statements against Us) — the Ayahs — ** أَنَّ مِنْكُمْ مُكَذِّبِينَ ** (that there are deniers among you), meaning so-and-so (فلاناً) and so-and-so (فلاناً). And وَإِنَّهُ لَحَسْرَةٌ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ (Indeed it is a regret for the faithless) means Ali (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi said: It means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Increase the recitation of Surat al-Haqqah, for its recitation in obligatory and supererogatory prayers is part of faith in Allah and His Messenger, because it was only revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and Mu'awiyah. And its reciter will not be stripped of his religion until he meets Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The like of it, without the saying: "because it was only revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and Mu'awiyah, upon whom be the abyss (الهاوية)."
